message:
Add pane splitter rendering primitives and layout lab integration

New host-agnostic abstraction for splitter/handle rendering that works
identically in terminal and web hosts:

pane_interaction.rs:
- PaneSplitterVisualState enum (Idle/Hover/Active) and
  PaneSplitterPrimitive struct with split ID, resize target, axis,
  rail/handle rects, and visual state
- pane_splitter_rail_thickness() — adaptive rail (2 cells for large
  viewports, 1 for small)
- pane_splitter_handle_span() — deterministic handle length from
  split rect major axis
- collect_splitter_primitives() — builds all splitter primitives from
  solved PaneLayout with Idle/Hover/Active state from pointer position
- Tests for adaptive thickness defaults and state transitions

layout_lab.rs:
- Change pane_active_gesture to Cell<> for interior mutability during
  render-time reads
- Add 5 Cell<> fields for splitter diagnostics
- Mouse routing tracks hover pointer, clearing on out-of-bounds move
- render_pane_preview_panel() calls collect/render splitter primitives
- Status bar and debug panel show splitter counts
- pane_splitter_cells() maps axis+state to themed RenderCell pairs
- render_splitter_primitives() via frame.draw_rect_filled()
- 5 tests covering hover/active diagnostics, reset behavior, and
  embedded mouse routing

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
